title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Independent Platform Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer ingress, gateway api 1.5.1, routing, tls, delegation, and status into a new Gateway API routing and delegation policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of Gateway API routing and delegation policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.
Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership.
Design Gateway addresses and listeners with protocol, port, hostname, namespace attachment, certificate reference, TLS mode, and conflict behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare service, ingress, implementation-specific annotations, gateway api, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, keep K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A lab Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance-1rezfr". Tailor its gateway-route.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance-1rezfr
  - competency: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: referencegrant status conformance
    environment: clean conformance cluster with only required add-ons installed
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: scheduler event eligible domains skew and placement result
    case-key: 1rezfr
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
